title: SceneTransitionPhases
description: A `{ enter, exit }` pair of independently-authored PhasedSceneTransition instances (spec §3.9.1/§3.10) — a union of two variants requiring at least one of `{ enter, exit }`, never an interface with both fields optional. An interface form would type-check `transition: {}` as valid, which — since a call-site value fully replaces the registry default (§3.10 rule 1) — would silently suppress a scene's configured default while looking like a no-op. Confirmed, TypeScript `--strict`: the union form correctly rejects `{}` (see `test/type-tests/scene-transition-phases.type-test.ts`).

sections.title: Import
paragraphs: A `{ enter, exit }` pair of independently-authored PhasedSceneTransition instances (spec §3.9.1/§3.10) — a union of two variants requiring at least one of `{ enter, exit }`, never an interface with both fields optional. An interface form would type-check `transition: {}` as valid, which — since a call-site value fully replaces the registry default (§3.10 rule 1) — would silently suppress a scene's configured default while looking like a no-op. Confirmed, TypeScript `--strict`: the union form correctly rejects `{}` (see `test/type-tests/scene-transition-phases.type-test.ts`).

members.name: SceneTransitionPhases
members.signature: { enter: PhasedSceneTransition; exit?: PhasedSceneTransition } | { enter?: PhasedSceneTransition; exit: PhasedSceneTransition }
